summary: Linked the Marill item translation unit by switching src/melee/it/items/itmaril.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The code changes in itmaril.c were focused on matching/link correctness: adding the missing ItemStateTable data object it_803F82B0, moving helper code earlier, and extracting repeated lifetime decrement logic into a static helper so function order and sdata2/data ordering match the original.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itmaril.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...), making the file participate as a matching object.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F82B0[] with four motion entries for itMaril_UnkMotion0 through itMaril_UnkMotion3, including anim, phys, and coll callbacks and one NULL collision callback for UnkMotion3.
Added static helper itMaril_UnkMotion2_Anim_inline(Item_GObj* gobj) that decrements xD44_lifeTimer and returns true when the timer reaches zero.
Replaced duplicated timer-decrement logic in itMaril_UnkMotion1_Anim and itMaril_UnkMotion2_Anim with calls to itMaril_UnkMotion2_Anim_inline.
Moved itMaril_UnkMotion1_Coll_inline earlier in the file and changed its declaration from static inline void to static void in the shown diff.
The PR body states the purpose directly: reorder functions for correct sdata2 ordering and add ItemStateTable to fill out a missing data section.
smart_moves: The PR treated matching as a whole-translation-unit problem, not just function code: adding the missing ItemStateTable data object was necessary before marking the object Matching.
Factoring identical lifetime logic into a local static helper let two animation functions share the same emitted code pattern while also supporting the needed function/data ordering.
The author explicitly adjusted function order to influence sdata2 ordering, a common decomp tactic when constants or small data placement block linking.
The configure.py change was made only after the item file had enough reconstructed data and ordering fixes to be promoted from NonMatching to Matching.
decomp_lessons: When an item file is close to matching but cannot be linked, check for missing state-table data objects in addition to nonmatching functions.
ItemStateTable reconstruction can be required to fill a missing data section and unlock object-level matching.
Function order in the C file can affect sdata2 ordering; moving or introducing static helpers may be a valid matching tactic when code is already semantically correct.
Repeated inline-looking logic may need to be represented as a shared static helper to reproduce the compiler's layout or call structure.
Promotion in configure.py from NonMatching to Matching should be paired with evidence that both text and data sections are accounted for.
naming_conventions: State-table symbol used the address-style name it_803F82B0, consistent with unresolved or address-derived data symbols.
Marill item callbacks followed the existing itMaril_UnkMotionN_Anim, itMaril_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itMaril_UnkMotionN_Coll naming pattern.

Local extracted helpers used the suffix _inline, as in itMaril_UnkMotion2_Anim_inline and itMaril_UnkMotion1_Coll_inline, to preserve the relationship to logic that was previously inline or duplicated.
The item is spelled itmaril/itMaril in paths and symbols, matching the existing project naming in the diff rather than the Pokémon's English spelling Marill.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added a concrete ItemStateTable array to account for missing data-section contents.
Reordered functions to obtain correct sdata2 ordering, according to the PR body.
Extracted timer-decrement code from two animation functions into a static helper and called it from both sites.
Moved the collision helper earlier in the source to influence emitted symbol/order behavior.
Changed the object status in configure.py to Matching once the source and data layout matched.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence beyond the title was the PR description: function reordering was for correct sdata2 ordering, and ItemStateTable was added to fill a missing data section.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs where adding an ItemStateTable allowed a file to become Matching.
Search for PRs mentioning sdata2 ordering and function reordering.
Compare itmaril.c with neighboring Pokemon item files such as itporygon2.c, ithinoarashi.c, and itfushigibana.c for similar state-table layout patterns.
Look for later cleanup that renames it_803F82B0 or resolves UnkMotion callback names.
